page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.medium is a general-purpose instance powered by a single-core AWS Graviton processor. Built on the ARMv9 architecture with Neoverse-V2 cores, this instance features 1 dedicated vCPU operating at 2.8 GHz, backed by 4.0 GB of system memory. It includes 59 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8gd.medium demonstrates average single-core CPU performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng single-core score of 1693.28 and Geekbench single-core score of 1735.0. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance is strong at 14.02 GB/sec. However, due to its single-core configuration, multi-core benchmarks are poor, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 1738.0. Memory bandwidth is relatively weak, with uncached large-block read speeds at 30.15 GB/sec and write speeds at 11.64 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8gd.medium offers a balanced entry-point resource profile for ARM-based workloads, combining dedicated CPU execution with high-speed local NVMe storage. The inclusion of bundled instance storage provides high-speed local scratch space without requiring external block storage. This instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and development or testing environments that benefit from ARM64 architecture and local SSD storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.medium is a general-purpose, ARM64-based instance powered by a single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 1 dedicated physical core, 4.0 GB of system memory, and 59 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 1735.0 and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance of 14.02 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory write bandwidth metrics are limited by its single-vCPU configuration. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight ARM64 development, single-threaded processing, and basic web serving workloads requiring fast local scratch space.

tagline: A single-core ARM64 instance featuring dedicated Graviton processing and local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight general-purpose workloads.

4.0 GB of system memory and 59 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Dedicated CPU allocation on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded decompression capabilities
Best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, ARM64 development, and testing
